# Hospital Acupuncture Implementation Framework (HAIF)

The Hospital Acupuncture Implementation Framework (HAIF) is a structured, evidence-based methodology for integrating acupuncture and acupressure into hospital practice. HAIF is a collaborative innovation between RMIT University and Northern Health, led by Professor Zhen Zheng, PhD (NHMRC Translating Research into Practice Fellow, App 1110446). HAIF guides clinicians and hospital administrators through four implementation phases based on the EPIS (Exploration, Preparation, Implementation, Sustainment) model. The primary application is post-operative nausea and vomiting (PONV), where PC6 acupoint stimulation has high-quality Cochrane-level evidence. HAIF has also been applied to PANDA — Personalised Acupuncture in the Northern Hospital Emergency Department for Acute Pain.

## About

- [Home](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/): Overview of the HAIF framework, key evidence summary, and audience-segmented entry points for administrators, clinicians, practitioners, and researchers.
- [About HAIF](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/about/): Background on the framework, its development at RMIT University, and the team behind it.
- [FAQ](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/faq/): Comprehensive answers to 40+ questions about hospital acupuncture including evidence, costs, training requirements, barriers, and getting started.

## Framework Phases

- [Framework Overview](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/framework/): The four-phase EPIS implementation cycle with navigation to each phase.
- [Phase 1: Exploration](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/framework/exploration/): Identifying internal needs, assessing PONV incidence, reviewing intervention modalities, forming the implementation team, surveying staff attitudes, evaluating organizational readiness (CARI checklist), and assessing costs.
- [Phase 2: Preparation](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/framework/preparation/): Developing implementation strategies, selecting timing and patient populations, revising hospital guidelines, addressing barriers (awareness, capability, equipment, time), and FAME criteria assessment.
- [Phase 3: Implementation](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/framework/implementation/): Executing the plan using CFIR domains, training staff with role-differentiated packages, engaging leadership, running pilot programs, and applying audit and feedback strategies.
- [Phase 4: Sustainment](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/framework/sustainment/): Normalizing practice into routine operations, fidelity monitoring, champion identification, continuous education, leadership reporting, and cost-effectiveness tracking.

## Evidence

- [Evidence Summaries](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/evidence/): GRADE-rated evidence for PC6 acupoint stimulation modalities including acupressure wristbands, manual acupressure, body acupuncture, electroacupuncture, electroacupressure, and auricular acupuncture. Based on Cochrane review by Lee et al. (2015).
- [References](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/references/): Complete bibliography including Cochrane reviews, EPIS model, CFIR framework, CARI readiness checklist, WHO acupuncture report, and 27+ peer-reviewed sources.

## Application Examples

- [Acupressure for PONV](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/examples/ponv-acupressure/): Nurse-led acupressure wristband implementation for post-operative nausea and vomiting at Northern Hospital, Victoria. Real-world case study with timeline and outcomes.
- [PANDA — Acupuncture in the ED](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/examples/panda/): Personalised Acupuncture in the Northern Hospital Emergency Department for Acute Pain. Research in progress — extends HAIF from peri-operative care into acute emergency medicine and addresses acupuncturist credentialling in a hospital setting.

## Resources

- [Resources](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/resources/): Downloadable tools including PONV risk self-assessment checklist, PONV data extraction template (Excel), Modified CARI readiness checklist, sample staff survey questions, patient survey templates, and cost assessment frameworks.
- [Contact](https://hospitalacupuncture.com/contact/): Contact form for questions about the HAIF framework and hospital acupuncture implementation.
